core_model.structure_summary: A repeated strategic interaction among randomly matched agents in which each agent's discounted continuation value satisfies a recursive optimality (incentive-compatibility) inequality, and the sustainable equilibria are characterized by bounds relating one-shot payoff gains to discounted value differences.

variables.meaning: record/type label carried by an agent into a match
discounted continuation value function satisfying a recursive optimality equation
action chosen each period, with a designated dominant action
discount factor (expected lifespan) and one-shot payoff differences

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A fixed-point (Bellman-type) condition in which a value function equals the discounted maximum over actions of current payoff plus expected continuation value, with incentive inequalities bounding payoff gains by discounted value gaps.

notes: Core is a repeated game with anonymous random matching and endogenous record disclosure; the operative mathematics is a recursive (Bellman/promise-keeping) value function plus incentive-compatibility inequalities yielding the anti-folk-theorem bounds. Random matching gives an average-of-others (mean-field) coupling. Stripped of the strategic/probabilistic dressing, the skeleton is the fixed-point value-maximization (dynamic-programming) condition with threshold incentive constraints.
